Now I'm on Level 2. Let me analyze the new level:

<function=python>
<parameter=code>

        print(f"  {node['color']} pixels={node['pixels']} center≈({cr:.0f},{cc:.0f}) hash={node['hash'][:8]}")
</parameter>
</function>

5. Dark gray endpoints (G pixels=12): [35,45] and [48,54], both with purple pixels

- Orange square at [12,24] with magenta
- Purple square at [41,49] with magenta
And there are TWO rings:

- Purple square at [41,49] needs to move toward the purple ring at [15-21, 54-59] (UP)
Wait, but the orange ring is at the bottom and the purple ring is at the right side. Let me think about this more carefully.

- Purple square should move toward purple ring (up)
Let me try clicking the magenta pixels to move the squares toward their respective rings.
For the orange square at [12,24]:
- To move toward orange ring at [48-54, 37-43], it needs to go DOWN and RIGHT
- But the magenta click moves the square in one direction. In Level 1, clicking magenta moved the square UP (toward the ring).
Wait, in Level 1, the ring was at the top and the endpoint was at the bottom. Clicking magenta moved the square UP toward the ring. So the direction is always toward the ring of the same color.
For Level 2:

- Purple square → purple ring (right side) → need to move RIGHT or UP
